sae_features.label: Alpha-helical coiled-coil scaffolds
sae_features.description: Alpha-helical coiled-coil scaffolding segments—including calponin-homology (CH) domains and heptad-repeat coiled-coils—found broadly in eukaryotic actin- and microtubule-associated proteins, ciliary/IFT components, spindle organizers, Rho-pathway scaffolds, and related signaling adaptors.

sae_features.label: Anion-binding adaptor loops
sae_features.description: Interface signal in eukaryotic adaptor modules for cytoskeleton, membrane trafficking, and small‑GTPase signaling—most commonly in Calponin‑homology (CH) and Phox homology (PX) domains, but also present in other anion‑engaging adaptor folds (e.g., PH/C2 and Ras‑association/Ras‑binding modules)—highlighting flexible basic/polar loop or helix‑turn segments that contact negatively charged partners (F‑actin, phosphoinositide headgroups, or acidic protein surfaces).

sae_features.label: Pro/Ser-rich low-complexity IDRs
sae_features.description: Long, intrinsically disordered low‑complexity tracts enriched in Pro/Ser with mixed acidic/basic residues that serve as flexible linker/tail regions in eukaryotic cytoskeleton‑associated proteins (microtubule/actin binders and scaffolds) and analogous plant low‑complexity regulators

sae_features.label: N-terminal CH/DCX cytoskeleton-binding
sae_features.description: Calponin-homology–like cytoskeleton-binding modules and closely related N‑terminal microtubule-contacting segments—i.e., the helix/loop surfaces at the start of CH or DCX folds and adjacent tails—found in MAPs, kinetochore/ciliary scaffolds, and actin/microtubule adaptors

sae_features.label: Regulatory IDR/linker motifs
sae_features.description: Regulatory, non-catalytic segments—predominantly intrinsically disordered, low‑complexity linkers and terminal tails enriched in Ser/Thr/Pro and acidic residues that carry short linear motifs (e.g., phosphorylation sites, localization signals) and flank folded domains (e.g., C2/PLAT, CH, EF‑hand, DNA‑binding), with little activation in catalytic cores.

sae_features.label: DH/Sec7 catalytic helical bundle
sae_features.description: An all‑alpha helical bundle fold corresponding to the catalytic cores of small‑GTPase guanine nucleotide exchange factors (primarily Dbl homology DH domains of Rho/Rac GEFs and Sec7 domains of ARF GEFs), with occasional cross‑activation on other large helical bundles such as the S1–S6 transmembrane cores of HCN-type ion channels.

sae_features.label: PX–UBC/AGC domain detector
sae_features.description: Domain-level detector for trafficking modules: it activates on the folded cores of PX (Phox homology) phosphoinositide-binding domains and on ubiquitin/UBL-conjugating E2 (UBC) catalytic cores, with sub-peaks over the PX C-terminal substructure and the E2 HPN motif/catalytic cysteine region; a secondary class of activators comprises AGC-like serine/threonine kinase catalytic domains.

sae_features.label: Amphipathic helical scaffold activation
sae_features.description: Amphipathic α-helical scaffold segments (often coiled-coils/BAR) and their adjacent S/P/T-rich linkers in eukaryotic proteins; enriched in membrane-trafficking/adaptor families but also present in other eukaryotic scaffolds (e.g., proteasomal receptors, nuclear regulators/enzymes).
